title: Networks
modulePurpose:
How to Explain Networks So Customers Feel Comfortable Saying Yes

Section Purpose
This section teaches agents how to confidently explain how networks work within the MightyWELL bundles in a way that makes customers feel comfortable, informed, and confident moving forward.
This is a sales module.
Customers often worry about networks because they are used to being restricted in traditional systems. Your job is to explain how this model is different — clearly, simply, and confidently.
Agents should leave this section understanding:
Members can go to any provider they choose
Networks make things easier, not restrictive
In-network care is simple and convenient
Out-of-network care is still fully accessible
Networks are optional tools, not barriers
The health share does not require a network for major health events
How to explain networks in plain English without sounding technical
The goal is simple:
Make networks sound easy, flexible, and stress-free.
Because when customers feel comfortable, they move forward.

moduleSteps.title: Step 1 – Review Overview
moduleSteps.subtitle: Review Overview
moduleSteps.copy:
The First Thing to Say in Any Network Conversation
Start here.
Always.
You can go to any doctor you want.
That sentence immediately removes fear.
Customers are used to being told:
"You must stay in-network."
 "You need approval."
 "You can't go there."
This model works differently.
Members are never trapped.
A simple sales line:
You can go wherever you feel most comfortable receiving care.

Why Networks Exist
Networks are not rules.
Networks are tools.
They exist to make healthcare:
easier
smoother
faster
more predictable
They help simplify billing and keep costs consistent, but they do not control where someone can go.
A strong one-liner:
Networks make healthcare easier — they don't control your choices.
Another:
Networks are there for convenience, not limitations.

The Networks Available
Members have access to large national provider networks, including:
PHCS MultiPlan
First Health
These are widely used networks with thousands of providers across the country.
But remember the key message:
Using the network is helpful — not required.
A simple way to explain it:
Think of the network like a fast lane, not a locked gate.

In-Network Care — How to Explain It
If the provider is in-network:
The process is very simple.
Show your member card
Pay your copay
Receive care
That’s it.
A strong sales explanation:
In-network is the easiest path — show your card, pay your copay, and you're done.
Another:
In-network is simple and predictable.

Out-of-Network Care — How to Explain It
If the provider is out-of-network:
The process is still easy.
Receive care
Pay for the visit
Upload the invoice through the app
Receive reimbursement minus your copay
That’s the workflow.
A simple sales explanation:
Out-of-network is not a problem — it's just one extra step.
Another:
You can still go anywhere — we just handle the paperwork a little differently.

What Customers Usually Worry About
Most customers ask:
"What happens if my doctor is not in-network?"
Your answer should be calm and confident.
Say:
No problem — you can still see your doctor.
Or:
You’re not stuck — you always have options.
Or:
We focus on flexibility, not restrictions.

Typical Visit Costs — Keep It Realistic
Most routine visits — such as:
urgent care
primary care
specialist visits
are generally manageable and predictable.
That makes out-of-network care easy to handle.
The message to communicate:
Out-of-network care is manageable, simple, and fully accessible.

How Networks Fit Into the Bundle
Remember the structure.
Every bundle has two parts.
Part one:
Everyday healthcare handled by third-party administered benefits.
Part two:
Protection for major health events handled by the health share.
Here is the key distinction:
Networks help with routine care.
Networks are not required for major health events.
For major health events handled through the health share:
There is no network limitation.
That flexibility is one of the strongest selling points in the entire model.
A strong one-liner:
For major health events, you can pursue the best care available — no network restrictions.

What Agents Should Never Say
Agents must never say:
"You have to stay in-network."
 "You can't go out-of-network."
 "This works exactly like traditional insurance."
 "You need permission to see a provider."
Those statements create confusion and reduce trust.

What Agents Should Say Instead
Use confident, simple language like:
"You can go to any doctor you want."
"Networks make things easier, not restrictive."
"In-network is the simplest option."
"Out-of-network is still fully accessible."
"You always have the freedom to choose your provider."
"For major health events, there are no network limitations."

The Big Sales Message
Flexibility builds trust.
Simplicity builds confidence.
Confidence closes sales.

moduleSteps.title: Step 2 – Review Materials
moduleSteps.subtitle: Review Materials
moduleSteps.copy:
Agents should review the network tools available in the portal.
This includes:
how to check if a provider participates in a network
how to explain in-network vs out-of-network
how to guide members to upload invoices
how networks apply to routine care
how networks do not apply to major health events
Agents should keep these materials open while completing the quiz.

moduleSteps.title: Step 3 – Quiz Questions
moduleSteps.subtitle: Take Quiz
moduleSteps.copy:
Module 4 – Networks
1. True or False
Members can go to any provider they choose.
Correct Answer: True

2. What is the best way to describe the purpose of networks to a customer?
A. They restrict where you can go
 B. They make healthcare easier and more convenient
 C. They prevent out-of-network care
Correct Answer: B

3. If a provider is in-network, what is the correct process?
A. Pay the full bill and submit paperwork
 B. Show your member card and pay your copay
 C. Call the network before the visit
Correct Answer: B

4. If a provider is out-of-network, what should the member do?
A. Cancel the visit
 B. Pay the bill and upload the invoice through the app
 C. Wait for approval
Correct Answer: B

5. True or False
Out-of-network care is still allowed and accessible.
Correct Answer: True

6. For major health events handled through the health share, members must stay within a network.
Correct Answer: False

7. Which statement is the best sales explanation of networks?
A. You must stay in-network
 B. Networks are there for convenience, not limitations
 C. Out-of-network care is not available
Correct Answer: B

8. True or False
The main message to customers about networks should be flexibility and choice.
Correct Answer: True
